name: The Delight of Discovery at Gandhi Bazaar
description:
In an age when everything arrives at our doorstep, what do we lose when we stop visiting markets?

Join chef and food researcher Keertida Phadke for a walk through Gandhi Bazaar in Basavangudi, exploring the market as a living landscape of food, memory, community and consumption. Together, we will explore the market in search of the small, specific delights that only presence can offer- a lady selling seasonal greens, a pushcart piled with fleshy red dates, a corner shop specialising in roasted snacks, the fragrance of champa flowers that transport you to another time and place.

Part exploration and part sensory exercise, this walk invites participants to think of the market as a living archive, about paying attention and rediscovering the value of serendipity in an increasingly frictionless world.

name: À la Carte: A Recipe Journaling Workshop
description:
Why do we turn to some recipes again and again? Why are there some tastes and textures that continue to bring comfort? 

Food has always been steeped in culture, history and memory. We pass our practices down from generation to generation, from friend to friend, household to household. Each time a dish is recreated, it takes a life of its own. Yet every repetition solidifies the ritual of sharing culinary knowledge.

This is an interactive workshop and discussion on Recipe Journaling, looking at the process of documenting traditional, borrowed and renovated recipes for yourself. Together, we will explore the significance of creating and maintaining personal artefacts and records, including a hands-on activity that helps you get started with your own recipe journal.

name: Small Portions: Making Miniature Food Sculptures
description: Food takes many forms in our lives, it can be the heart of a social gathering and a vessel for old memories. Join Neha Hegde to celebrate the foods that hold a special place in our lives through crafting miniature food sculptures. Learn how to sculpt foam clay, engaging with prompts and games themed on our exhibition season CALORIE. Relax and unwind as we reflect on our fraught but intimate relationship with food.
